name: London
localTips: Just tap a contactless card or phone on the Tube and buses — it's automatically capped at the daily travelcard price.
Stand on the right on escalators. Londoners are serious about this one.
All the big national museums — British Museum, Tate, Natural History, V&A — are completely free.
Pubs stop serving food earlier than you'd expect (often 21:00) — eat before you settle in for the evening.
A 'Sunday roast' at a proper pub is the essential London meal — book ahead, the good ones sell out by noon.

activities.title: South Bank riverside walk
activities.description: Follow the Thames from Westminster Bridge past the London Eye, skate park graffiti and book stalls to Tower Bridge — the city's best free promenade.

activities.title: British Museum (free)
activities.description: The Rosetta Stone, the Parthenon sculptures and two million years of history under Norman Foster's spectacular glass roof — all free.

activities.title: Tate Modern (free)
activities.description: World-class modern art in a colossal former power station — the Turbine Hall installations alone are worth the trip, plus a free 10th-floor viewing terrace.

activities.title: Natural History Museum (free)
activities.description: Dinosaurs, a blue whale skeleton suspended over the grand hall, and the earthquake simulator — in one of London's most beautiful buildings.

activities.title: Hyde Park & Kensington Gardens
activities.description: Rent nothing, spend nothing: swans on the Serpentine, Speakers' Corner debates on Sundays and the Peter Pan statue among 350 acres of green.

activities.title: Camden Market
activities.description: A maze of 1,000 stalls in Victorian stables — street food from every continent, vintage leather, and canal-side people-watching.

activities.title: Greenwich Park & the Meridian line
activities.description: Stand astride the Prime Meridian, then take in the skyline view down to the Old Royal Naval College — arrive by riverboat for the full effect.

activities.title: Columbia Road Flower Market
activities.description: Every Sunday this East End street disappears under flowers and cockney traders' banter — come for the shouting, stay for the coffee shops.

activities.title: Sky Garden (free, book ahead)
activities.description: A tropical garden and 360° viewing gallery on the 35th floor of the Walkie-Talkie — London's best free skyline view, rain or shine.

activities.title: Changing of the Guard
activities.description: Bearskins, brass bands and precision drill outside Buckingham Palace — check the schedule, it's not every day in winter.

activities.title: Notting Hill & Portobello Road
activities.description: Pastel townhouses, the world's largest antiques market on Saturdays, and the bookshop from the film — best explored with no plan at all.

activities.title: National Gallery (free)
activities.description: Van Gogh's Sunflowers, Turner's seascapes and 700 years of European painting overlooking Trafalgar Square — dip in for an hour, it's free.

activities.title: Hampstead Heath & Parliament Hill
activities.description: Wild woodland, swimming ponds and the classic skyline view from Parliament Hill — London's countryside, 20 minutes from King's Cross.

activities.title: St Dunstan in the East garden
activities.description: A bombed-out medieval church swallowed by vines and fig trees — the City's most atmospheric hidden garden, minutes from the Tower.

activities.title: Maltby Street Market
activities.description: Bermondsey's railway-arch food market — smaller, better and far less mobbed than Borough, with gin distilleries hiding in the arches.
activities.insiderNote: Saturdays only for the full market; go hungry and graze — the grilled cheese at The Cheese Truck is the local vote.

activities.title: God's Own Junkyard neon wonderland
activities.description: A warehouse crammed floor-to-ceiling with vintage neon signs from Soho's heyday — part gallery, part café, entirely surreal.
activities.insiderNote: Weekends only. Combine with the craft breweries on the same Walthamstow industrial estate — locals call it 'Blackhorse Beer Mile'.

activities.title: Leake Street graffiti tunnel
activities.description: A legal graffiti tunnel under Waterloo station where the walls change daily — you'll often catch artists mid-piece.
activities.insiderNote: It's fully covered, making it one of London's best rainy-day photo spots. Evenings feel edgier but the light is better.

activities.title: Daunt Books Marylebone
activities.description: An Edwardian bookshop with oak galleries and skylights, where books are shelved by country — the most beautiful browse in London.
activities.insiderNote: The travel section at the back is the original heart of the shop — pick up a novel set wherever you're heading next.

activities.title: Candlelit evening at Gordon's Wine Bar
activities.description: London's oldest wine bar (1890) — a candlelit cave under Embankment where you drink sherry from the barrel among crumbling vaults.
activities.insiderNote: No reservations for the cave — arrive before 17:30 on weekdays to snag a vault table, then order the cheese board.

activities.title: Midnight beigel on Brick Lane
activities.description: Beigel Bake has served salt-beef beigels 24 hours a day since 1974 — the classic end to an East London night out.
activities.insiderNote: Order the salt beef with mustard and gherkin, cash-fast queue moves quicker than it looks. Under £7.

activities.title: Little Venice canal walk to Camden
activities.description: Follow the Regent's Canal towpath from Little Venice's moored narrowboats through Regent's Park to Camden — 50 minutes of waterside calm.
activities.insiderNote: You pass right by London Zoo's Snowdon Aviary — free glimpses of the birds from the towpath.

activities.title: Same-day West End rush tickets
activities.description: Most West End theatres release same-day 'rush' tickets from £10–25 via the TodayTix app or box-office lotteries — big shows, small prices.
activities.insiderNote: The TKTS booth on Leicester Square is the official discounter; matinees have the best availability.

activities.title: Tower of London & Crown Jewels
activities.description: A thousand years of executions, ravens and the Crown Jewels — take the free Yeoman Warder tour, the Beefeaters are natural comedians.
activities.bookingQuery: Tower of London entry ticket with Crown Jewels

activities.title: London Eye ride
activities.description: Thirty minutes of slow-motion skyline from the giant riverside wheel — Westminster looks best from 135 metres up around golden hour.

activities.title: Westminster Abbey
activities.description: Coronations since 1066, Poets' Corner and the tombs of kings, queens and Newton — the audio guide by Jeremy Irons is genuinely excellent.

activities.title: Borough Market food tour
activities.description: Graze through London's 1,000-year-old food market with a guide — oysters, curing rooms, and the best toastie in the city.
activities.bookingQuery: Borough Market guided food tour London

activities.title: Harry Potter Warner Bros. Studio Tour
activities.description: The actual Great Hall, Diagon Alley and butterbeer — a half-day pilgrimage northwest of London that sells out weeks ahead.
activities.bookingQuery: Harry Potter Warner Bros Studio Tour London with transport

activities.title: Thames speedboat blast
activities.description: A theatrical RIB ride from Tower Bridge to Canary Wharf at 35 knots, with a soundtrack — the fun-not-formal way to see the river.

activities.title: Classic afternoon tea
activities.description: Finger sandwiches, scones and a tiered stand of pastries — from grand hotels to quirky bus-top tea tours, peak London indulgence.

activities.title: Jack the Ripper evening walking tour
activities.description: Gaslit alleys of Whitechapel and the unsolved 1888 case, told by expert guides with Victorian police photos — deliciously creepy after dark.
activities.bookingQuery: Jack the Ripper evening walking tour London

activities.title: St Paul's Cathedral & dome climb
activities.description: Whisper across Wren's gallery, then climb 528 steps to the Golden Gallery for a panorama that beats any skyscraper's.
activities.bookingQuery: St Pauls Cathedral entry and dome climb ticket London

activities.title: Hyde Park Winter Wonderland
activities.description: Britain's biggest Christmas fair — ice skating, bratwurst stalls, roller coasters and mulled-wine chalets filling a corner of Hyde Park.

activities.title: Kew Gardens in bloom
activities.description: The world's greatest botanic garden — Victorian glasshouses, a treetop walkway and 50,000 plants, at its heady best in late spring and summer.
